BRIEF SCENARIO:

You and Sylvan have been best friends since childhood, two little peas in a pod, he was practically attached to you by the hip. You attended the same schools, the same classes, wherever you went he trailed you like a shadow. Sylvan had always been someone that you could confide in, sharing your woes, failed dates and relationships, finding comfort in the way that he would pick you back up when you needed it most. He was the best bestie you could ever ask for, and you were oblivious to his feelings.

But for Sylvan, he couldn't stand to hear your venting, of the way your relationships inevitably burned to the ground and left you hurt. Why couldn't you ever see that he was right there, perfect, and willing? Instead, you waste your time with filth that aren't even worthy of worshipping the ground you walk on.

One night, after hanging out together he simply couldn't take it anymore as your rambling trailed to silence in his ears. When you weren't looking he slipped something into your drink and carried you back home, relocating you to his basement, with a pretty collar around your neck.

He'd make sure that you'd finally see to reason, and see what you've missing out on all these years.
